behaviors.description: The content passed to the summary-generation step includes data retrieved from all three sources.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is module handoff: three source collectors -> aggregation/context builder -> summary-generation call payload. MOCK-MISS: this is not a single-function check; omission bugs appear when modules are wired. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) no real OS/network/subprocess required to reproduce payload-composition bug; in-process fakes for the three source clients are sufficient, so not System. (b) assertion is about input payload completeness, not model output quality, so not Agentic. (c)(d) no multi-agentic chain, so not Workflow. REJECTION-GATE: rejected System/Agentic because behavior text is specifically about "content passed to the summary-generation step".
behaviors.test_description: Wire real data-collection and report-assembly modules with stubbed source clients returning distinct sentinel fields per source. Execute one run up to the summary-generation invocation and assert the exact payload passed downstream contains all three source sections/keys.

behaviors.description: When one data source is unavailable, the report is produced with a `data unavailable` note for that section rather than aborting.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is cross-module error propagation and fallback rendering (source adapter failure -> report assembler continues -> section note injected). Unit is too low because this is handoff behavior. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) real network/OS not required; a client stub raising timeout/error reproduces the failure mode, so not System. (b) correctness here is fallback control flow and note insertion, not LLM judgment quality, so not Agentic. (c)(d) no multi-agentic sequence, so not Workflow. REJECTION-GATE: higher levels disqualified by behavior text "rather than aborting" and explicit fallback note requirement.
behaviors.test_description: Run pipeline with one source client configured to fail deterministically and two sources succeeding. Assert execution completes, report artifact exists, failed source section contains `data unavailable`, and no global abort/exception escapes.

behaviors.description: The narrative summary accurately represents the key values present in the input data.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on model output fidelity (fact preservation / semantic accuracy), which deterministic stubs cannot validate. LLM-DEP applies directly: this behavior is about what the summary says, not orchestration around the call. ADJACENT TIE (System vs Agentic): concrete higher-level-only failure is hallucinated or numerically incorrect summary despite correct plumbing. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Workflow because behavior text describes one generation step ("The narrative summary...") rather than multiple model decisions in sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Execute summary generation against the real model/API on representative fixtures with known key values, then score outputs against factual checks (key metric values, issue counts, deployment outcomes) with tolerance/coverage criteria across repeated runs to capture non-deterministic drift.

behaviors.description: A complete report — from data collection through email delivery — reaches the configured recipient's inbox correctly.
behaviors.justification: This is end-to-end delivery through real external email/network/inbox infrastructure. REAL-INFRA and MOCK-HIDE: in-process email mocks can pass while real SMTP/API auth, network, MIME formatting, or provider acceptance fails. Not Agentic as minimum because pass/fail criterion is delivery to inbox; summary content quality is not the asserted behavior. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Agentic/Workflow because behavior text is "reaches ... inbox correctly" (transport outcome), not model-decision quality or multi-agentic chain.
behaviors.test_description: Run the scheduled script in an environment with real reachable data endpoints (or controlled test endpoints) and a real test mailbox. Verify message arrives in configured recipient inbox with expected subject/body attachment structure and run metadata.

behaviors.description: When the email delivery service is unreachable, the generated report is saved to a local file and delivery is retried on the next scheduled run.
behaviors.justification: Behavior spans real failure/recovery boundaries: unreachable email service + local filesystem persistence + subsequent scheduled execution retry. REAL-INFRA: OS filesystem semantics and actual delivery endpoint unreachability are central; mocks can hide permission/path/locking/timing issues. INTEGRATION-BRAKE check fails at (a): real OS/network/scheduling effects are part of the behavior, so Integration is too low. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Agentic/Workflow because behavior text is infrastructure retry/fallback, not LLM output quality or multiple model decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Force real email endpoint failure (e.g., invalid route or blocked service) during run 1; assert report is persisted to expected local path. On run 2 after restoring email service, assert persisted report (or queued equivalent) is retried and successfully delivered, with retry state cleared.
